Name: Chocolatería San Ginés
Tips: Open 24 hours 365 days since 1894. Madrid's most iconic churros institution. Order porras (thick churros) + thick chocolate for dipping. White-tiled interior unchanged for decades. Night-owl and early-morning favourite.

Name: Café Comercial
Tips: Madrid's oldest café (est. 1887). Marble tables, high ceilings, mirrors, proper literary atmosphere. Order café con leche + tostada con tomate. Beloved by madrileños across all ages.

Name: La Musa
Tips: Trendy Malasaña brunch café — excellent eggs dishes, good tortilla. One of Madrid's best-regarded brunch spots. Crowded on weekends. Huevos rotos (broken eggs over jamón and fries) is the standout.

Name: HanSo Café
Tips: Best flat white in Madrid. Exceptional croissants, Scandi-influenced aesthetic. Queue on weekend mornings. Kouign-amann croissant is unmissable.

Name: Casa Dani
Address: Mercado de la Paz C/ de Ayala 28 28001 Madrid (Salamanca)
Tips: Legendary tortilla bar inside Mercado de la Paz. Queues form before it opens. World-benchmark tortilla española — creamy, slightly undercooked in the centre (jugosa). Cash only. Arrive at opening (09:00).

Name: La Casa del Abuelo
Address: C/ de la Victoria 12 28012 Madrid (near Sol)
Tips: Famous for gambas al ajillo (prawns in garlic olive oil) since 1906. Order house sweet red wine alongside. Dive straight in at opening — queues grow fast. Four locations across Madrid.

Name: Cava Baja
Address: Calle de la Cava Baja 28005 Madrid (La Latina)
Tips: La Latina's main tapas street — densest and best concentration of traditional tapas bars in Madrid. Bar-hop 4–5 bars ordering 1–2 tapas + drink each. Best 17:00–19:00 or after 21:00 (local timing).

Name: Carambola
Tips: Family-friendly tapas restaurant with good kids' menu, relaxed atmosphere, and creative tapas. A reliable option when travelling with children. Patatas bravas, pimientos de padrón, pan con tomate.

Name: Bodegas Ricla
Tips: Ancient wine bar (est. 1860) near La Latina — one of Madrid's oldest. Dark, dusty, barrel-aged wines served straight from the barrel. Embutidos and cheese. Step back in time. House wine €1.50–2/glass.

Name: El Brillante
Tips: Institution near Atocha station for bocadillo de calamares — Madrid's signature street food: crusty roll filled with fried calamari rings. The quintessential cheap Madrid snack. Pair with cold Mahou beer.

Name: Flamenco de Leones
Address: C/ de los Leones Centro Madrid
Tips: Flamenco dinner show — tapas + dinner + live performance. Earlier sitting (20:00–21:00) works best with children. Book ahead in August. Intense and passionate show.

Name: Cardamomo
Tips: One of Madrid's best-regarded authentic flamenco tablaos. Strong dancers, serious musicians, good dinner menu. More upscale than tourist-oriented shows. Book ahead.

Name: Sobrino de Botín
Tips: Guinness World Records oldest restaurant (est. 1725). Wood-fired oven cochinillo asado (roast suckling pig) in a medieval cellar. A Madrid institution — touristy but the food is genuinely exceptional. Book 1–2 weeks ahead.

Name: Taberna El Sur
Address: C/ de la Torrecilla del Leal 12 28012 Madrid (La Latina)
Tips: Beloved La Latina neighbourhood restaurant — simple, unpretentious, serious food. Best in Madrid for cocido madrileño (chickpea-and-meat stew served in stages). Reserve cocido ahead — limited daily.

Name: Mercado de San Miguel
Address: Plaza de San Miguel 1 28005 Madrid (La Latina)
Tips: Stunning early 20th-century iron and glass market hall near Plaza Mayor. Stalls serve jamón, oysters, pintxos, vermouth, croquetas, patisserie. Arrive at 10am before crowds. Best pre-dinner vermut spot.

Name: Bar Cock
Tips: One of Madrid's most storied cocktail bars (1921). Dark mahogany interior, bow-tied bartenders, timeless. Classic cocktails — Manhattan, Negroni, Sidecar. Old-world elegance.

Name: 1826 Dry Bar
Tips: Serious gin-and-tonic bar in Malasaña — obsessively curated gin selection and tonic pairings. Madrid has extraordinary gin-tonic culture; this is one of the best places to experience it. Let them choose for you.
